J1.basis: Lumina pivoted from an old product ("InboxZap," an email-cleanup tool) to an AI meeting assistant, but the technical migration was never finished ... GSC showing 12 already-indexed post URLs pulling thousands of impressions ... every single existing post targets the *old* email-cleanup audience; zero content targets Lumina's actual buyers
J1.rationale: Explicitly names the pivot, treats the inbox-cleanup GSC history as legacy demand, and frames every fix around the meeting-assistant reality; falls just short of anchor_10 only because it doesn't separately flag the repo itself as partially stale.

J2.basis: kept because they still carry real search impressions and evergreen utility ... post-7 (a near-duplicate of post-3) now 301s here ... GSC showing 12 already-indexed post URLs pulling thousands of impressions
J2.rationale: Makes a couple of real per-asset decisions (301 post-7→post-3, cross-link two adjacent legacy posts) but treats the other ten as an undifferentiated 'keep' with only an aggregate impressions figure — no asset-by-asset dispositions grounded in individual impressions/positions from the export.

J3.basis: 301'd post-7 into post-3 ... Added two cornerstone posts (AI meeting notes, meeting action items) and lightly cross-linked from the two most thematically adjacent legacy posts
J3.rationale: Cleanly avoids both failure modes — preserves salvageable legacy equity via redirect/keep/cross-link while building the meeting-assistant cluster as the growth engine — but the tension and transition sequencing are implied through actions rather than explicitly articulated.

J1.basis: Lumina pivoted from an old product ("InboxZap," an email-cleanup tool) to an AI meeting assistant, but the technical migration was never finished... every single existing post targets the *old* email-cleanup audience; zero content targets Lumina's actual buyers
J1.rationale: Explicitly names the pivot, correctly reads the live site as the meeting assistant and the legacy blog as pre-pivot demand, and frames the content strategy (new cornerstone posts for the real ICP) around that reality. Falls just short of 10 only because it doesn't distinctly characterize GSC-as-legacy-demand vs repo-as-stale as separate signals.

J2.basis: GSC showing 12 already-indexed post URLs pulling thousands of impressions... post-3 and post-7 were both 'bulk delete emails'... 301'd post-7 into post-3... lightly cross-linked from the two most thematically adjacent legacy posts
J2.rationale: Makes a couple of real dispositions (301 the duplicate, keep+cross-link two adjacents, keep the rest) but treats impressions only in aggregate ('thousands') with no per-asset or tiered read of the actual export numbers; the other ~9 posts get a blanket 'keep as-is' with no data justification.

J3.basis: They're kept because they still carry real search impressions and evergreen utility... Added two cornerstone posts... and lightly cross-linked from the two most thematically adjacent legacy posts
J3.rationale: Cleanly avoids both traps — doesn't torch legacy equity and doesn't double down on inbox content — preserving/redirecting salvageable posts while building the meeting-assistant cluster as the growth engine; loses points for only implicitly stating the tension and providing little explicit sequencing of the transition.

J1.basis: Lumina pivoted from an old product ("InboxZap," an email-cleanup tool) to an AI meeting assistant, but the technical migration was never finished... every single existing post targets the *old* email-cleanup audience; zero content targets Lumina's actual buyers
J1.rationale: Explicitly names the pivot, identifies the live product as the meeting assistant and the blog as legacy inbox demand, and frames every fix around that reality; loses a point only because it treats the pivot as a technical-migration fact rather than dwelling on the GSC-as-legacy-demand distinction in words.

J2.basis: GSC showing 12 already-indexed post URLs pulling thousands of impressions... post-3 and post-7 were both 'bulk delete emails'... 301'd post-7 into post-3... lightly cross-linked from the two most thematically adjacent legacy posts
J2.rationale: Makes real dispositions (301 the duplicate, keep-as-is the rest, cross-link two adjacents) and cites the 12 URLs, but there is no asset-by-asset or tiered walk grounded in actual per-post impressions/positions — the default 'keep everything else' is applied without invoking each post's numbers.

J3.basis: They're kept because they still carry real search impressions and evergreen utility... Added two cornerstone posts (AI meeting notes, meeting action items) and lightly cross-linked from the two most thematically adjacent legacy posts
J3.rationale: Cleanly avoids both traps — preserves legacy equity rather than torching it, builds the meeting-assistant cluster as the growth engine, and bridges authority via cross-links; falls short of a fully sequenced transition plan with an explicit tension articulation, keeping it just below the top anchor.
